title: Make or Buy
event: Reyes Co. makes a part for $16 per unit: direct materials $4, direct labor $3, variable overhead $2, and $7 of allocated fixed overhead of which only $1 is avoidable. A supplier offers the part for $11. Compute the relevant cost to make and determine the decision.

computationPaths.narration: Only costs that DISAPPEAR if you buy are relevant. The unavoidable $6 of fixed overhead stays either way — it can't vote.

memorize.body: Relevant make cost = variable costs + AVOIDABLE fixed costs. Compare to purchase price.
The $16 'full cost' says buy; the $10 relevant cost says make. Allocated-but-unavoidable overhead flips decisions when handled wrong.

questions.prompt: DM $4, DL $3, VOH $2, avoidable FOH $1, unavoidable FOH $6. Relevant cost to make = ?

distractors.feedback: That's FULL cost including the unavoidable $6 — which continues whether you make or buy. Relevant cost is 10.
You dropped the avoidable $1 of fixed overhead. Costs that disappear if you buy ARE relevant: 10.
